steps.purpose: If the user gave a partial ticker or natural-language name, resolve to the exact series_ticker via the local SQLite substring index. Skip this step when the user already passed a full ticker (e.g. KXMENWORLDCUP-26 vs the literal substring 'world cup').
steps.failure_modes: Requires a prior `prediction-goat-pp-cli sync` to populate kalshi_series; on a fresh install this returns count:0. Fall back to `kalshi series list --limit 50 --agent | grep -i <substring>`.
steps.skip_when: query.tickers contains a complete KX[A-Z0-9]+ token already

steps.purpose: List every event under the series. The `--series` flag forces `--data-source live` because the local store doesn't index events by series ticker. Use `--limit 25` to keep response under ~5KB; raise if the user asked for a long-tail series.
steps.failure_modes: Returns empty when the series has no active events. Check status field — closed/settled series should still surface here so the agent can answer historical questions.

steps.purpose: For the highest-volume active event from the previous step, fetch every child market with live prices in one call. `--with-markets` passes `with_nested_markets=true` to the upstream /events/{ticker} endpoint; the response includes ticker, yes_sub_title, status, yes_ask_dollars, no_ask_dollars, volume_24h_fp, expiration_time per child.
steps.failure_modes: Long-tail child markets can carry the platform-default 17c ask with zero volume; filter those out with the untraded heuristic (`last_price_dollars == 0 && volume_24h_fp == 0 && (yes_ask + no_ask) > 1.10`) before quoting probabilities.

steps.purpose: Build the summary: series ticker + title + count of active events + top-5 markets by 24h volume across all active events, each rendered as title + yesPercent + volume24h.
